questions.q: By what affectionate nickname is the Hindi film music director R. D. Burman popularly known?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman was popularly nicknamed Pancham.

questions.q: R. D. Burman was the son of which famous Hindi film music director?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman was the son of the music director S. D. Burman.

questions.q: R. D. Burman composed the music for which blockbuster 1975 multi-starrer directed by Ramesh Sippy?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman composed the music for the 1975 film Sholay.

questions.q: Which female playback singer, who later became R. D. Burman's wife, was one of his most frequent collaborators?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle was a frequent collaborator of R. D. Burman and later became his wife.

questions.q: Which male playback singer formed a celebrated partnership with R. D. Burman, singing many of his hit songs?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar formed a celebrated partnership with R. D. Burman.

questions.q: R. D. Burman scored the music for which 1971 Dev Anand film centred on hippie culture, featuring the song 'Dum Maro Dum'?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman composed the music for the 1971 film Hare Rama Hare Krishna.

questions.q: In which decade is R. D. Burman especially noted for modernising Hindi film music with rock and Western elements?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman is especially noted for modernising Hindi film music in the 1970s.

questions.q: R. D. Burman's final film score, for which he won posthumous acclaim, was for which 1994 film?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman's acclaimed final score was for the 1994 film 1942: A Love Story.

questions.q: What was R. D. Burman's primary profession in the Hindi film industry?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman worked primarily as a music director in Hindi cinema.

questions.q: R. D. Burman blended which kinds of Western musical styles with Indian melody to create his signature sound?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman blended rock, funk, Latin and electronic elements with Indian melody.

questions.q: In which year was R. D. Burman born?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman was born in 1939.

questions.q: In which year did R. D. Burman pass away?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman died in 1994.

questions.q: Which 1966 Nasir Hussain film, starring Shammi Kapoor, is regarded as R. D. Burman's breakthrough as an independent music director?
questions.explanation: Teesri Manzil (1966) is regarded as R. D. Burman's breakthrough as an independent composer.

questions.q: What was R. D. Burman's full given name?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman's full name was Rahul Dev Burman.

questions.q: Which lyricist had an especially prolific collaboration with R. D. Burman on songs such as those in Aandhi and Kinara?
questions.explanation: Gulzar collaborated extensively with R. D. Burman, including on Aandhi and Kinara.

questions.q: R. D. Burman won his first Filmfare Award for Best Music Director for which 1982 film?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman won his first Filmfare Best Music Director award for Sanam Teri Kasam (1982).

questions.q: Which Rajesh Khanna film of 1972, composed by R. D. Burman, featured the hit songs 'Chingari Koi Bhadke' and 'Kuch To Log Kahenge'?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman composed the music for the 1972 film Amar Prem.

questions.q: In which city was R. D. Burman born?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman was born in Kolkata (then Calcutta).

questions.q: Before becoming an independent composer, R. D. Burman served as a music assistant to which person on films like Pyaasa and Guide?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman assisted his father S. D. Burman, including on Pyaasa and Guide, before going independent.

questions.q: Which 1968 film composed by R. D. Burman gave Kishore Kumar the comic yodelling song 'Mere Saamne Wali Khidki Mein'?
questions.explanation: 'Mere Saamne Wali Khidki Mein' is from Padosan (1968), composed by R. D. Burman.

questions.q: R. D. Burman's nickname 'Pancham' is most commonly explained by what association with the musical scale?
options: His baby cry was likened to the fifth note
questions.explanation: A popular story holds that as a baby his cry resembled the fifth note (Pa/Pancham), giving rise to the nickname.

questions.q: Which percussionist served as R. D. Burman's chief rhythm assistant, helping craft the beats of his songs from his earliest films?
questions.explanation: Maruti Rao Keer, a noted tabla and percussion player, was R. D. Burman's chief rhythm assistant.

questions.q: R. D. Burman's first film as an independent music director was the 1961 film Chhote Nawab, secured for him by which comedian-actor?
questions.explanation: Mehmood gave R. D. Burman his first independent assignment with Chhote Nawab (1961).

questions.q: R. D. Burman won his second Filmfare Award for Best Music Director for which 1983 Shekhar Kapur film featuring 'Tujhse Naraz Nahin Zindagi'?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman won his second Filmfare Best Music Director award for Masoom (1983).

questions.q: R. D. Burman won his third and final Filmfare Best Music Director award posthumously for 1942: A Love Story. Which lyricist wrote its songs?
questions.explanation: Javed Akhtar wrote the lyrics for 1942: A Love Story, for which R. D. Burman won posthumously.

questions.q: Which song from 1942: A Love Story, sung by Kumar Sanu, became one of R. D. Burman's last great hits?
questions.explanation: 'Ek Ladki Ko Dekha' from 1942: A Love Story, sung by Kumar Sanu, was among R. D. Burman's last hits.

questions.q: R. D. Burman was known for unconventional sounds; in 'Mehbooba Mehbooba' from Sholay, he himself performed which role?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman sang the lead vocals on 'Mehbooba Mehbooba' from Sholay.

questions.q: Which Subhash Ghai film scored by R. D. Burman featured the hit song 'Om Shanti Om' sung by Kishore Kumar?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman composed the music for Karz (1980), known for 'Om Shanti Om'.

questions.q: R. D. Burman's mother, Meera Dev Burman, was associated with which creative role in S. D. Burman's work?
questions.explanation: Meera Dev Burman, R. D. Burman's mother, was a lyricist who wrote for S. D. Burman's compositions.

questions.q: R. D. Burman composed the rock-tinged song 'Bachna Ae Haseeno', picturised on Rishi Kapoor, for which 1977 Nasir Hussain film?
questions.explanation: 'Bachna Ae Haseeno' is from Hum Kisise Kum Naheen (1977), composed by R. D. Burman.
